Also need to have an image of objective0 high_res. That requires taking an image with a calibrated camera that supports high_res mode, and then communicating image data.

We have cameras: camera0 on rover1, supports colour, high_res. camera1 on rover0, supports colour, low_res. camera2 on rover0, supports low_res.

Goal wants communicated_image_data objective0 high_res. That means we need to have an image of objective0 with mode high_res. So we can take an image of objective0 from any waypoint, as long as we are at that waypoint and the camera is calibrated and supports high_res mode, and we have enough energy.

We need to calibrate the camera before taking image. calibrate action requires calibration_target ?i ?t (camera i calibration_target objective t) and (in ?r ?w) and (visible_from ?t ?w) and (on_board ?i ?r) and (equipped_for_imaging ?r) and (>= energy 2).
